labels.notes: ACTUAL PATIENT: Evelyn Horvath (telephone visit 9:51am, 2nd of 3 back-to-back phone calls). Metadata + saved name says 'Samuel Aran Mezhenchik' (DOB 1984-12-26) — WRONG, that's the NEXT patient. SOAP, dx 724 (lumbago), and A102A all clinically correct for Evelyn — only the patient identity is wrong. DEFECT (NEW Class A — vision chart pre-load): pipeline_log shows 22 vision calls cycling through 6 chart states across 8.8 min: Shrikrishna lingering (3 votes 13:52-54) → Ruth Doherty transient (1 vote 13:55) → Evelyn Horvath the actual patient (3 votes 13:56-58, DOB 1951-09-16) → NOT_FOUND (2 votes 13:59-00) → Samuel Menhinick the next patient's chart pre-loaded (10 votes 14:01-14, DOB 1984-12-28). Recency-weighted majority elected Samuel because doctor opened Samuel's chart ~3 min before Evelyn's call ended. v0.10.45 DOB-mismatch invalidation DID fire at the Evelyn→Samuel transition but only clears votes once — couldn't beat 10 subsequent Samuel votes. This is the SYMMETRIC failure mode to the Apr 20 Shelley/Richard case (which was prev-patient lingering). Suggested fixes: (a) freeze name vote once K=5 streak reached + ignore later contradictions; (b) compute majority on first-half-of-encounter when ≥3 distinct names ever seen; (c) add `vision_calls_after_dob_invalidate_cap` so post-invalidation votes can't dominate. Per the user's 'no vision for session detection' rule, vision identity is post-hoc audit only — but this case is patient-name metadata which IS meant to use vision.
